mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in lower corporate profit growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates a structural shift in aggregate demand; when housing construction accelerates while consumer credit stagnates, it signals a rotation into rate-sensitive sectors that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of the variance in 60-day forward returns, or the signal direction flips consistently during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its long-term trend while inflation remains contained, it reflects benign supply-side capacity expansion that supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO prints above trend, or the signal loses predictive power during supply chain disruption episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP acts as a fundamental earnings proxy, where positive GDP acceleration directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or GDP growth decelerations consistently precede positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5%, it signals an aggressive tightening cycle that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite rates exceeding 2.5%, or the forward return differential between high-rate and low-rate periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate determines labor market tightness; when payroll additions exceed 150,000 while unemployment remains below 4.0%, it indicates a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 2-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction term shows negative predictive power during labor market cooling phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in consumer credit growth relative to historical averages signals impending demand weakness, prompting defensive positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite sustained credit contraction, or the signal generates a negative Sharpe ratio over any 12-month rolling window.
